admin.skillGraph.overview:
The skill graph is the curriculum backbone: nodes are discrete teachable competencies (grammar structures, functional-language chunks, vocabulary sets, subskills), and edges are prerequisite relationships between them — a true dependency order, not just a CEFR label.

Nodes are AI-drafted from CEFR frameworks and existing content, then reviewed and approved here in batches, never one at a time. Approved nodes are the sole curriculum unit driving Today, Practice Gym, and per-student mastery tracking — the old flat Curriculum-objective system has been fully retired.

Typical admin workflow on this page: review pending nodes and approve or reject in batch, check the coverage heatmap for CEFR level x skill gaps, draft nodes to fill a gap, and sweep untagged Modules to link real content to approved nodes.
admin.skillGraph.sweepUntaggedModules:
Sweeping AI-matches up to 20 untagged approved Modules per run against approved nodes sharing their CEFR level/skill, and auto-applies every validated match.

This targets Modules that have never been tagged at all — it is a separate pass from the coverage list below, so a run reporting "0 links applied" can still be working correctly if none of this batch's Modules matched an approved node.
admin.skillGraph.nodeCoverage:
Darker cells have more approved nodes. The amber badge marks pending nodes awaiting review.

Click a gap cell (zero approved nodes) to draft nodes for that CEFR level and skill.
admin.skillGraph.draftNodes:
Triggers one bounded AI call proposing 2-5 competency nodes.

Every proposed CEFR level, skill, and subskill is validated against the real taxonomy — an AI hallucination is dropped, never applied.
